action.reasoning: Observation: the recovered frozen-CLIP MLP still gives ~1.4% val accuracy after full-data training, so despite tiny-subset overfitting the full pipeline remains effectively random. Analysis: the most likely issue is not optimization anymore but representation mismatch from using CLIP vision features without the exact CLIP image preprocessing; CLIP expects resize/crop and especially conversion through its image processor conventions. Since our images are already 224x224, the key remaining mismatch is likely interpolation/normalization/token path, and a stronger route is to use the official CLIPProcessor-derived normalization plus a simpler linear head on precomputed features to eliminate dataloader/model interaction bugs. We need a fast, reliable diagnostic before another long run. Decision: rewrite model/train/predict around offline precomputed CLIP image features using CLIPModel.get_image_features on batches, cache train/val features to disk, train a scikit-leliable logistic-regression-style linear classifier in PyTorch on cached features, evaluate locally on val, and submit only if clearly non-random. This is a shorter iteration than end-to-end training and directly tests whether CLIP features are informative.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 15 --lr 0.03 --train_batch_size 4096 --feature_batch_size 256 --checkpoint_path checkpoints/linear_probe_cache.pt && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/linear_probe_cache.pt --eval_split val
